build_check.description: The project launches with the headless command, exits with code 0, and produces no fatal errors. Returns 0 or 1; the rest of the score is multiplied by this gate.

requirements.description: The player brews potions at a cauldron by selecting ingredients and combining them. Known recipes show required ingredients. Successful brewing produces a potion that is added to shop shelves. Score 0 if there is no brewing mechanic, if ingredients cannot be combined, or if potions appear without crafting. Score 1 requires the mechanic to be presented in a visually authored context with real sprites or illustrated assets. Score 0.5 at most if the mechanic works but is represented entirely by programmatic shapes, solid-color fills, or default Godot widgets.

Customers enter the shop with visible ailments (icons or symptoms), browse shelves, and buy matching potions. Customers who find their needed potion pay gold; those who do not leave unhappy. Score 0 if customers do not appear, if they have no ailments, or if purchases do not generate gold. Score 1 requires the mechanic to be presented in a visually authored context with real sprites or illustrated assets. Score 0.5 at most if the mechanic works but is represented entirely by programmatic shapes, solid-color fills, or default Godot widgets.

The player sets prices for potions. Higher prices yield more gold per sale but reduce customer satisfaction. Lower prices attract more customers. Price changes have observable effects on sales volume. Score 0 if prices cannot be set or if price has no effect on customer behavior. Score 1 requires the mechanic to be presented in a visually authored context with real sprites or illustrated assets. Score 0.5 at most if the mechanic works but is represented entirely by programmatic shapes, solid-color fills, or default Godot widgets.

Ingredients are consumed when brewing and must be restocked by purchasing from a supplier menu. A gold economy tracks income from sales and expenses from restocking. Score 0 if ingredients are infinite, if restocking is free, or if there is no economy. Score 1 requires the mechanic to be presented in a visually authored context with real sprites or illustrated assets. Score 0.5 at most if the mechanic works but is represented entirely by programmatic shapes, solid-color fills, or default Godot widgets.

The full loop connects: title -> shop day (brew/sell/restock) -> day end summary -> next day. A reputation system tracks customer satisfaction over time. The game shows weekly results. Score 0 if any transition is missing or if there is no day cycle. Score 1 requires the mechanic to be presented in a visually authored context with real sprites or illustrated assets. Score 0.5 at most if the mechanic works but is represented entirely by programmatic shapes, solid-color fills, or default Godot widgets.

At least four potion types exist (healing, curing, buffing, antidote) with different sprites, ingredients, and customer demand. Score 0 if fewer than three potion types exist or if types are visually identical. Score 1 requires content variants to be visually distinct with real authored art (different sprites, illustrated elements). Score 0.5 at most if variants exist but are represented by programmatic shapes, color swaps, or text labels without real asset differences.

At least five ingredient types exist with different sprites and uses across recipes. Some ingredients are common and cheap; others are rare and expensive. Score 0 if fewer than three ingredients exist or if all ingredients are identical. Score 1 requires content variants to be visually distinct with real authored art (different sprites, illustrated elements). Score 0.5 at most if variants exist but are represented by programmatic shapes, color swaps, or text labels without real asset differences.

Customer types vary in ailment, budget, and patience. At least three customer types exist with different visual appearances and needs. Score 0 if all customers are identical or if only one ailment type exists. Score 1 requires content variants to be visually distinct with real authored art (different sprites, illustrated elements). Score 0.5 at most if variants exist but are represented by programmatic shapes, color swaps, or text labels without real asset differences.

Shop upgrades are purchasable (larger shelves, faster cauldron, ingredient garden, better decor) that improve capacity or efficiency. At least three upgrades exist. Score 0 if no upgrades exist or if the shop never improves. Score 1 requires content variants to be visually distinct with real authored art (different sprites, illustrated elements). Score 0.5 at most if variants exist but are represented by programmatic shapes, color swaps, or text labels without real asset differences.

New recipes can be discovered through experimentation (trying ingredient combinations) or purchased from recipe books. The recipe collection grows over time. Score 0 if all recipes are known from the start or if no discovery system exists. Score 1 requires content variants to be visually distinct with real authored art (different sprites, illustrated elements). Score 0.5 at most if variants exist but are represented by programmatic shapes, color swaps, or text labels without real asset differences.

The HUD shows gold, reputation, current inventory (ingredients and potions), and day/time in clear non-overlapping regions. Score 0 if gold, reputation, or inventory is missing. Score 1 requires the information to be presented through themed, styled UI with real assets. Score 0.5 at most if readability relies on plain text, unstyled labels, or raw geometric shapes.

The shop view clearly shows the cauldron, shelves with potions, ingredient cabinet, customer counter, and customers. Different areas are visually distinct and non-overlapping. Score 0 if shop areas are indistinguishable or if customers are invisible. Score 1 requires the information to be presented through themed, styled UI with real assets. Score 0.5 at most if readability relies on plain text, unstyled labels, or raw geometric shapes.

Actions produce visible feedback: brewing shows cauldron animation and potion creation, customers show purchase or disappointment, restocking shows ingredients added, and price changes show updated labels. Score 0 if actions produce no observable change. Score 1 requires the information to be presented through themed, styled UI with real assets. Score 0.5 at most if readability relies on plain text, unstyled labels, or raw geometric shapes.

Overall art coherence: shop interior, potions, ingredients, customers, cauldron, and UI belong to a consistent cozy-magical apothecary visual language with warm, rich tones. Score 0 if the screen mixes incompatible styles or reads as plain default Godot grey. Score 0.5 at most if the game uses programmatic shapes (ColorRect, solid fills, or untextured geometry) instead of real illustrated or pixel-art assets.

Potion and ingredient sprites are real illustrated or pixel-art objects (colorful vials, herbs, crystals, mushrooms). Different types are visually distinct and recognizable. Score 0 if potions or ingredients are plain colored shapes. Score 0.5 at most if the game uses programmatic shapes (ColorRect, solid fills, or untextured geometry) instead of real illustrated or pixel-art assets.

The shop interior uses real illustrated backgrounds with shelves, wooden surfaces, hanging herbs, and atmospheric lighting. The cauldron is a detailed illustrated object. Score 0 if the shop is a flat colored background. Score 0.5 at most if the game uses programmatic shapes (ColorRect, solid fills, or untextured geometry) instead of real illustrated or pixel-art assets.

HUD and UI use styled themed widgets with magical-shop chrome: recipe cards have illustrated parchment frames, price tags have hand-written styling, and menus have wooden-shelf backgrounds. Score 0 if UI elements are unstyled Godot defaults. Score 0.5 at most if the game uses programmatic shapes (ColorRect, solid fills, or untextured geometry) instead of real illustrated or pixel-art assets.

Animation and atmosphere: cauldron bubbles, potions glow on shelves, customers walk and browse, ingredients sparkle, and the shop has ambient magical effects (floating particles, flickering candles). Score 0 if all elements are static. Score 0.5 at most if the game uses programmatic shapes (ColorRect, solid fills, or untextured geometry) instead of real illustrated or pixel-art assets.
